title: BROWT
text:
An educational research project
BROWT is, above all, an educational research project. The goal is not a finished tool but to study — hands on — the hard problems behind an agent that plays Brawl Stars from the screen alone, with no access to the game’s internal state.
Two problems are deliberately pushed in depth, because they are the most challenging and the most instructive.

title: BROWT
text:
1. Perception — detecting objects in the frame
Identifying the relevant objects (brawlers, gems, …) in each frame. Public datasets for this game are limited, so a large part of the work is studying and comparing techniques that push detection accuracy as far as possible, with all training and inference kept local — no cloud services.


2. Decision — acting on unlabeled information
Choosing what to do from the perceived state, without labels telling us the correct action. This is a reinforcement-learning problem: the agent must learn a policy from its own experience and a reward signal.

title: BROWT
text:
How this documentation is organized
The documentation mirrors these two axes:

Detection and analysis — the perception problem above.
Reinforcement learning — the decision problem above.

Each part is split into its own sections, added as the work progresses. A final part presents the general pipeline that ties everything together — deliberately placed last, once both halves are understood.

title: Detection and analysis
text:
This part covers the perception problem: detecting the relevant objects in each frame and turning them into usable information. Because public datasets for the game are limited, the focus is on techniques to maximize detection accuracy, with all training and inference kept local.






NoteWork in progress



The sections of this part will be added as the work progresses.

title: Reinforcement learning
text:
This part covers decision-making from unlabeled information: learning a policy that maps the perceived state to an action, with no labels telling us the right move — only experience and a reward signal.






NoteWork in progress



The sections of this part will be added as the work progresses.

title: General pipeline
text:
The pipeline under study
This final part ties the two halves together — perception (detection and analysis) and decision (reinforcement learning) — into the full system. It comes last on purpose, once both problems are understood on their own.
The agent is organised along three stages that turn a frame into an action.
flowchart LR
    subgraph SIM[1. Capture]
        A[Emulator] --> B[Screen capture]
    end
    subgraph DET[2. Perception]
        C[Object detection<br/>YOLOv11] --> D[State restructuring]
    end
    subgraph AI[3. Decision]
        E[RL policy]
    end

    B -->|frame| C
    D -->|state| E
At run time the stages form a closed perception-action loop: each action changes the game, producing the next frame, and the change in frames is the reward signal that trains the policy.
